children.heading: Evaluation of Justice Department Comprehensive Opioid Abuse Grant Program
children.label: 1 Evaluation of Justice Department Comprehensive Opioid Abuse Grant Program
children.text: Not later than 5 years after , the Attorney General shall complete an evaluation of the effectiveness of the Comprehensive Opioid Abuse Grant Program under part LL of title I of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 [ et seq.] administered by the Department of Justice based upon the information reported under paragraph (4).July 22, 201634 U.S.C. 10701

children.heading: Interim evaluation
children.label: 2 Interim evaluation
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , the Attorney General shall complete an interim evaluation assessing the nature and extent of the incidence of opioid abuse and illegal opioid distribution in the United States.July 22, 2016

children.heading: Metrics and outcomes for evaluation
children.label: 3 Metrics and outcomes for evaluation
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Attorney General shall identify outcomes that are to be achieved by activities funded by the Comprehensive Opioid Abuse Grant Program and the metrics by which the achievement of such outcomes shall be determined.July 22, 2016

children.heading: Metrics data collection
children.label: 4 Metrics data collection
children.text: The Attorney General shall require grantees under the Comprehensive Opioid Abuse Grant Program (and those receiving subawards under section 3021(b) of part LL of title I of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 []) to collect and annually report to the Department of Justice data based upon the metrics identified under paragraph (3).34 U.S.C. 10701(b)

children.heading: Publication of outcomes and metrics
children.label: A Publication of outcomes and metrics
children.text: The Attorney General shall, not later than 30 days after completion of the requirement under paragraph (3), publish the outcomes and metrics identified under that paragraph.

children.heading: Publication of evaluation
children.label: B Publication of evaluation
children.text: In the case of the interim evaluation under paragraph (2), and the final evaluation under paragraph (1), the entity conducting the evaluation shall, not later than 90 days after such an evaluation is completed, publish the results of such evaluation and issue a report on such evaluation to the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives and the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate. Such report shall also be published along with the data used to make such evaluation.

children.label: A
children.text: enter into an arrangement with the National Academy of Sciences; or

children.label: B
children.text: enter into a contract or cooperative agreement with an entity that is not an agency of the Federal Government, and is qualified to conduct and evaluate research pertaining to opioid use and abuse, and draw conclusions about overall opioid use and abuse on the basis of that research.
